data.title: U.S. home prices rose by 0.2% in November from the previous month, with a 2.6% increase from a year earlier according to Redfin.
data.description: In Seattle, U.S. home prices saw a 0.2% month-over-month increase in November on a seasonally adjusted basis, slightly down from October's 0.3% rise. Data from a recent report by Redfin, the real estate brokerage backed by Rocket, showed a 2.6% year-over-year increase, a bit slower than the previous month's 2.9% growth. Home-price growth has been decelerating throughout the year, with November recording the smallest year-over-year increase so far.

data.title: RxWellness COO Phoebe Casey named to WPO Women2Watch 2025 list for leading fast-growing healthcare company. #HERNDON, Va, Dec. 23.
data.description: One of the nation's fastest-growing women-led healthcare companies, RxWellness Spine & Health, based in Herndon, Virginia, highlighted its Co-Founder and Chief Operating Officer, Phoebe Casey, who was recognized for her contributions. The announcement was made on December 23, 2025. Casey's achievement was acknowledged through her appointment to the Women's Presidents organization. This recognition underscores RxWellness' impressive growth trajectory and commitment to fostering female leadership in the healthcare sector.

data.title: Bybit launches Mantle Vault in partnership with Mantle and Cian, for stablecoin returns, the second-largest exchange.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, in Dubai, UAE, Bybit, the second-largest cryptocurrency exchange globally in terms of trading volume, collaborates with Mantle and Cian to introduce Mantle Vault on Bybit On-Chain Earn. This product is a stablecoin on-chain yield asset management offering, designed to provide users with increased opportunities for passive income generation in the cryptocurrency market.
ISRAEL DEFENCE MINISTER KATZ SAYS ISRAEL GOVERNMENT HAS NO INTENTION OF ESTABLISHING SETTLEMENTS IN GAZA

data.title: Ark Invest's Cathie Wood sells more Tesla stock as Q4 deliveries near, shifting to crypto. Market unbothered despite stock highs for self-driving bets.
data.description: Ark Invest, led by Cathie Wood, is reallocating funds towards cryptocurrency investments while Tesla stock continues to soar on market expectations of self-driving technology advancements, despite overlooking delivery statistics. Wood has recently divested more Tesla shares, with fourth-quarter delivery figures awaiting announcement, although market sentiment appears largely indifferent to this key data.
SPOT SILVER CROSSES $70/OZ FOR THE FIRST TIME

data.title: South Korean BC Card finishes trial enabling foreign individuals to use stablecoins for local merchant payments.
data.description: A successful pilot program by South Korean payments giant BC Card has enabled foreign users to make payments to local merchants using stablecoins.

data.title: Dow Jones falls as Novo stock soars on FDA approval for weight loss pill; Live coverage on Investor's Business Daily.
data.description: Today, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline in anticipation of the release of Q3 GDP data. Additionally, Novo stock saw a significant increase following FDA approval of a weight loss pill. The market update covered the decrease in the Dow Jones index and the exceptional performance of Novo stock due to the recent FDA approval, as reported by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Global market for refrigerated display cases valued at $9.2B in 2024, projected to reach $13.4B by 2030, growing at 6.4% CAGR. Business report offers analysis and forecasts.
data.description: A report on Refrigerated Display Cases has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The global market for these cases was worth US$9.2 billion in 2024 and is estimated to reach US$13.4 billion by 2030, with a projected growth rate of 6.4% from 2024 to 2030. The report offers a detailed analysis of market trends, drivers, and forecasts to assist in making informed business choices.

data.title: Jabil Inc. surpasses Q1 earnings, reports $8.31B revenue, and predicts $32.4B net revenue for FY 2026. Stock potential for $500 monthly.
data.description: Jabil Inc. (NYSE: JBL) reported robust fiscal first-quarter 2026 results, surpassing expectations. They announced quarterly adjusted earnings per share of $2.85 on December 17, beating the estimated $2.70. Their quarterly net revenue of $8.31 billion marked an 18.7% Y/Y increase, surpassing the projected $8.09 billion. The company expects fiscal 2026 net revenue to reach $32.40 billion, higher than the previous forecast of $31.30 billion and the analyst consensus of $31.523 billion. Adjusted EPS is anticipated to be $11.55, up from the previous guidance of $11.00, compared to the consensus estimate of $11.11. With an annual dividend yield of 0.14% or a quarterly dividend of 8 cents, investors are considering potential gains from Jabil. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
MannKind shares latest updates on FUROSCIX® business, including sales numbers and expansion plans.

data.title: Foresight partners with Zeda Korea in South Korea for its 3D perception systems, a strategic cooperation agreement.
data.description: On December 23, 2025, Foresight Autonomous Holdings Ltd. revealed a significant collaboration with Zeda Korea Ltd. The agreement, involving Foresight's advanced 3D perception systems, is set to unfold across multiple phases. Foresight, renowned for its innovative technologies, is listed on both Nasdaq and TASE under the ticker symbol FRSX. Zeda Korea, a prominent South Korean company specializing in road safety and infrastructure maintenance vehicles, will leverage Foresight's expertise in this partnership. This strategic cooperation underlines a major step for both companies in the realm of cutting-edge technology.

data.title: Totaligent (OTCID:TGNT) concludes 2025 with full marketing platform launch after successful QA testing across all channels. CEO shares holiday message.
data.description: Totaligent (OTCID:TGNT) has achieved a significant milestone by successfully finishing QA testing on its complete marketing and campaign platform. This marks a crucial step forward for the company in ensuring the quality and reliability of its services.

data.title: SiriusPoint expands IMG services post Assist America acquisition; strengthening global insurance benefits.
data.description: Bermuda-based company SiriusPoint Ltd. (NYSE: SPNT), a global specialty insurer and reinsurer, revealed on December 23, 2025, their acquisition plans. The company, along with its subsidiary International Medical Group (IMG), a prominent provider of global insurance benefits and assistance services, will acquire Assist America. This move solidifies SiriusPoint's position in the industry and expands its portfolio.

data.title: Qolab ends 2025 with strategic partnerships, milestones in quantum hardware, aiming for a commercially useful quantum computer.
data.description: In 2025, Qolab, headquartered in Madison, Wisconsin, concluded the year with significant achievements in strategic partnerships, product implementations, and industry recognition, highlighting the increasing support for its scalable superconducting quantum technology. These developments signal important steps towards the company's goal of constructing a practical quantum computer through advanced semiconductor-based manufacturing and holistic system integration. Over the year, Qolab strengthened its partnerships and solidified its position as a leader in the field.

data.title: Enveda receives US FDA clearance for IND application of ENV-6946, starting Phase 1 trial for oral treatment of IBD.
data.description: Enveda, a biotech company based in Boulder, Colorado, has recently received clearance from the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for their Investigational New Drug application for ENV-6946. This marks a significant step for the company as they have now initiated a Phase 1 clinical trial for ENV-6946. This oral small molecule drug is a first-in-class treatment aimed at addressing inflammation. Enveda is pioneering the development of small-molecule drugs derived from life’s chemistry, and this clearance from the FDA is a crucial milestone in their journey. The Phase 1 clinical trial will be a key stage in advancing ENV-6946 towards potential approval, showcasing Enveda's commitment to innovation in the biotech industry.

data.title: Volato Group (NYSE: SOAR) expects $27-28 million revenue in Q4 2025 and $78-79 million for the full year. Strategic growth noted.
data.description: Atlanta-based Volato Group, Inc. (NYSE American: SOAR) has released preliminary financial guidance for the fourth quarter and full year ending December 31, 2025. The company projects revenue between $27 million and $28 million for the fourth quarter of 2025, showcasing progress towards strategic and balance sheet goals. The anticipated revenue for the full year 2025 is expected to range from $78 million to $79 million. This reflects Volato's commitment to meeting its objectives and driving growth in the upcoming year.

data.title: Sharon AI to sell 50% of Texas Critical Data Centers LLC for US$70 million to joint venture partner New Era Energy & Digital Inc.
data.description: SharonAI Holdings Inc. and its subsidiaries have entered into a binding agreement to sell their 50% stake in Texas Critical Data Centers LLC (TCDC) for US$70 million. TCDC, a joint venture with New Era Energy & Digital Inc., will see Sharon AI's portion acquired by their partner. SharonAI is a prominent Neocloud company in Australia, traded as SHAZ:OTC Markets and SHAZW:OTC Markets.

data.title: VOKA offers free 1-month access to VOKA 3D Anatomy & Pathology for new users, a high-precision atlas for medical professionals.
data.description: A company based in St. Petersburg, Florida, VOKA, is offering a promotion where individuals can receive one month of free access to their VOKA 3D Anatomy & Pathology tool. This tool is a detailed 3D atlas designed for medical education and for professionals working in clinical settings. This offer allows users to utilize the high-precision 3D anatomy resources provided by VOKA for free for a limited period.

data.title: J.S. Held's Michelle Avery and Carey Miller awarded for leadership, innovation, and service in JERICHO, N.Y. Dec. 23, 2025.
data.description: In Jericho, New York, on December 23, 2025, Michelle J. Avery and Carey Miller, renowned forensic accounting and litigation consulting experts, were commended by global consulting firm J.S. Held for their exceptional leadership, innovation, and service. Their accomplishments were acknowledged in a press release issued by the firm.
